project_summary:
Per-project QA Workspace: route /project/:id with a Product|feature scope selector and five read-only tabs (Summary with on-demand deploy verdict, Test Cases, Traceability, Bugs, Artifacts) that surface the full evidence Aitri already produces — the layer that turns the Hub from a monitor into a QA management surface.

Product Analysis:
  North Star KPI: a QA auditor answers 'which TC failed, which MUST FR is uncovered, what is this bug's resolution' in ≤2 clicks from the overview card, without opening an editor or terminal.
  JTBD: 'When I audit an Aitri project, give me the test cases, traceability, bugs and specs as readable evidence so I can sign off or reject with confidence.'
  Guardrail metric: the overview page and its 5s collection cycle stay untouched — no added per-cycle process, dashboard.json additive-only.

NFR category applicability: Observability — new endpoints log through the existing web.js request logging (parent NFR); Healthcheck — unchanged (/health exists); CI/CD and Security addressed below; Performance addressed below (detail reads + on-demand validate).

functional_requirements.title: Card navigation to the per-project detail route
functional_requirements.description: Clicking a project card on the overview navigates to /project/:id (client-side route in the existing useRoute mechanism). The view has a back affordance returning to the overview with its state intact. An unknown or unregistered id renders a not-found state with a link back — never a blank page or crash.
acceptance_criteria: Click on a project card navigates to /project/<registered id> and the detail view renders that project's name in the header strip in ≤200ms after dashboard data is already loaded (client-side route, no full page reload)
Browser back (and the in-app back affordance) returns to the overview, which still renders all cards at the 375px, 768px and 1440px viewports
/project/does-not-exist renders a not-found state with a working link to '/' — no console error, no blank page

functional_requirements.title: Detail header strip
functional_requirements.description: The detail view shows a persistent header strip: project name, derived status badge, deploy-verdict chip (populated after the on-demand validate of FR-054; shows 'not checked' before), aitri version (full pre-release tag), and artifactsDir (mono).
acceptance_criteria: Header renders name, status badge, aitriVersion with full tag (e.g. 2.0.0-rc.159), and artifactsDir; all readable at the 375px viewport without horizontal scroll
Before validate has run, the verdict chip shows a neutral 'not checked' state (no fabricated verdict)
Header remains visible when switching tabs with no re-fetch flicker (single detail payload; tab switch re-render completes in ≤200ms)

functional_requirements.title: Localhost-only per-project detail endpoints
functional_requirements.description: The existing web.js server gains read-only endpoints serving per-project detail (exact route shape is a phase-2 ADR; the examples here are illustrative): a detail endpoint (e.g. GET /api/project/:id/detail?scope=...) returning the parsed artifact chain for the scope (test cases, results, traceability, requirements, bugs, artifact list+contents), and a validate endpoint running aitri validate --json on demand. Both enforce the existing remoteAddress loopback guard (403 otherwise). All file reads go through the rc.159 readers (resolveArtifact, layoutBase) and are restricted to the known artifact-name whitelist — no path is built from URL input beyond the registered project id and a validated scope name.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/project/<id>/detail from loopback returns 200 with JSON containing the scope's parsed artifacts; a simulated non-loopback remoteAddress receives 403 with no file read performed
A scope parameter containing path separators, '..', or a name not present in the project's features list is rejected with 400 and no filesystem access outside the project root
Only whitelisted artifact filenames are ever opened (verified by fs instrumentation in tests); a request naming anything else returns 400
GET /api/project/<unknown-id>/detail returns 404 with a JSON error body

functional_requirements.title: Scope selector: Product and feature sub-pipelines
functional_requirements.description: The detail view offers a scope selector listing Product plus every feature under the project's features dir (resolved via layoutBase). Selecting a scope re-renders Test Cases, Traceability, Bugs and Artifacts from that scope's own artifact chain (features/<name>/<artifactsDir>/ and its BUGS.json). Summary always shows the product view plus the per-feature indicator table.
acceptance_criteria: A project with 2 features shows 3 scope options (Product, f1, f2); selecting f1 renders Test Cases from features/f1's 03/04 artifacts (verifiable with distinct fixture data per scope)
Scope switching does not reload the page (client-side state) and re-requests only the detail payload for the new scope
A project with no features shows no selector (Product implied) and all tabs read the root chain

functional_requirements.title: Summary tab: health score, phases, feature table, on-demand deploy verdict
functional_requirements.description: Summary renders: the project's health score/grade (same derivation the overview card uses, shown for the selected project), per-phase pipeline status for the selected project (approved/completed/drifted per phase, from the collected record), the per-feature indicator table (phase, verify state, tests passed/total, open bugs per feature), and a deploy-verdict panel populated by GET /api/project/:id/validate — the aitri validate --json result rendered per the VALIDATE_JSON.md contract: verdict, blocking reasons and advisories, each with its suggested command (mono). validate runs when the panel is first opened and on an explicit refresh button — NEVER in the 5s collection cycle; the endpoint result is cached server-side for 60s to absorb repeated tab switches.
acceptance_criteria: The Summary tab shows the project's health score/grade consistent with the value the overview card computes for the same collected record
Opening Summary on a validate-capable project renders the verdict plus each blocking reason and advisory with its command, matching the fixture's validate --json output
The 5s collection cycle spawns zero validate processes (fs/process instrumentation over 3 cycles with the detail view closed AND open)
The refresh button re-invokes validate at most once per click; two tab switches within 60s serve the cached result (1 spawn total)
A project whose aitri CLI is unavailable renders a degraded verdict panel naming the failure — no crash, no fabricated verdict

functional_requirements.title: Test Cases tab
functional_requirements.description: Table joining 03_TEST_CASES.json with 04_TEST_RESULTS.json for the selected scope: TC id (mono), title, automation (auto/manual with manual_reason on hover or expand), scenario, latest status (pass/fail/pending/skipped), evidence reference when present, downgraded_from audit trail when present, linked FR id and AC id. Filters: by status, by automation, by linked FR. A counts strip (passed/failed/pending/skipped/manual). Pending manual TCs are called out in a prominent banner (they block coverage until verified).
acceptance_criteria: A fixture scope with 5 TCs (1 fail, 1 pending manual) renders 5 rows with correct statuses; the counts strip shows 3/1/1 and the manual-pending banner names the pending TC id
Filtering by status=fail shows exactly the failing TC; filtering by linked FR shows only that FR's TCs; filters compose
A TC with downgraded_from renders the downgrade trail; a manual TC exposes its manual_reason
With 03_TEST_CASES.json present but 04_TEST_RESULTS.json absent, every TC renders status 'pending' and the empty-state note names 'aitri verify-run' (no crash)

functional_requirements.title: Traceability tab
functional_requirements.description: One row per FR of the selected scope (id mono, title, priority MUST/SHOULD/NICE) with its linked TCs and each TC's latest result, built from 05_TRACEABILITY.json and the results' fr_coverage. Uncovered MUST FRs (no passing test) are pinned at the top with a red marker. ac_coverage is shown per FR where present. The intent-coverage map (01_REQUIREMENTS.json#coverage_map) renders as its own section (need → disposition) with the requirements-audit freshness stamp (fresh/stale/not-run) derived from the collected record.
acceptance_criteria: A fixture with one uncovered MUST FR renders it pinned first with a red uncovered marker; covered FRs list their TCs with per-TC latest status
coverage_map entries render need→disposition; a disposition naming an FR links/scrolls to that FR's row
The audit freshness stamp shows 'stale' for a fixture whose requirements changed after the last audit (per the record's coverage-audit fields) and 'not run' when absent
With 05_TRACEABILITY.json absent, the empty state names 'aitri run-phase 5' / the traceability artifact — no blank panel

functional_requirements.title: Bugs tab
functional_requirements.description: Table from the selected scope's BUGS.json: id (mono), title, severity, status, blocking flag, resolution text, files_changed, linked TC. Blocking bugs (critical/high, active) are pinned at the top with a red band. A corrupt BUGS.json renders the parse-error state inline (scope named, 'bugs are NOT counted') — never an empty table presented as zero bugs.
acceptance_criteria: A fixture with 3 bugs (1 blocking with resolution) renders the blocking bug pinned with red band, resolution text readable, linked TC shown
A corrupt BUGS.json fixture renders the inline parse-error state naming the scope; no '0 bugs' presentation
An absent BUGS.json renders the empty state ('no bugs recorded — aitri bug add') distinct from the corrupt state

functional_requirements.title: Artifacts tab
functional_requirements.description: Rendered reading view of the selected scope's chain. Markdown artifacts (00_DISCOVERY.md, 01_UX_SPEC.md, 02_SYSTEM_DESIGN.md, 04_CODE_REVIEW.md, AUDIT_REPORT.md) render as formatted markdown. 01_REQUIREMENTS.json renders as a human PRD projection (FR table with id/title/priority/ACs, personas, no_go_zone) with a collapsible raw-JSON view; other JSON artifacts get a summary header plus the collapsible raw view. Artifact list shows presence/absence per chain position.
acceptance_criteria: 02_SYSTEM_DESIGN.md renders with headings/lists/code blocks formatted (not raw text); the raw markdown is NOT shown by default
01_REQUIREMENTS.json renders the FR table with id, title, priority and ACs plus personas and no_go_zone; the raw JSON is available under a collapsed toggle
The chain list marks absent artifacts as such (e.g. 00_DISCOVERY.md 'not produced — optional phase') instead of omitting them silently
A markdown artifact containing raw HTML/script content renders inert text — no script execution, no HTML injection (sanitized or escaped rendering)

functional_requirements.title: Empty and degraded states on every tab
functional_requirements.description: Every tab renders an explanatory empty/degraded state when its source artifact is absent, malformed, or the scope is mid-pipeline: the state names the missing artifact AND the aitri command that produces it. A degraded snapshot (CLI too old / failed) shows the existing degradation banner on the detail view. No tab ever renders a blank panel or throws.
acceptance_criteria: For each of the 5 tabs, a fixture missing that tab's artifact renders a non-empty state naming the artifact and its producing aitri command (5 assertions); each state is visible at the 375px viewport
A malformed artifact (invalid JSON) renders the degraded state naming the file — no crash, no blank panel, zero uncaught exceptions in the console
The detail view of a snapshot-degraded project shows the degradation banner with the required CLI version

user_personas.goal: Sign off or reject a release from readable evidence: which tests passed, what covers each MUST requirement, what every blocking bug's resolution was
user_personas.pain_point: Today the Hub shows only counters; the evidence lives in JSON files they must open in an editor

user_personas.goal: See which TCs are pending/failed and what each manual TC requires, per feature
user_personas.pain_point: No per-TC view exists; manual TCs and their reasons are invisible

user_personas.goal: Read the PRD, UX spec and design docs of any project without cloning it or opening an editor
user_personas.pain_point: Markdown and JSON artifacts are unreadable for non-developers in raw form

user_stories.i_want: to open a project's detail from its card
user_stories.so_that: I can drill into evidence without leaving the Hub

acceptance_criteria.given: the overview shows a card for registered project 'demo'
acceptance_criteria.then: the URL is /project/<demo-id> and the detail header shows 'demo'
a not-found state with a link to '/' renders; no blank page, no console error

user_stories.i_want: a header strip with identity, status and verdict
user_stories.so_that: I always know which project and state I am judging

acceptance_criteria.then: the header shows the full tag 2.0.0-rc.159 and the verdict chip reads 'not checked' until validate runs

user_stories.i_want: the detail data served only to my machine
user_stories.so_that: project evidence never leaks off localhost

acceptance_criteria.when: GET /api/project/<id>/detail arrives with a non-loopback remoteAddress
acceptance_criteria.then: the response is 403 and no artifact file was opened
the response is 400 and no filesystem access outside the project root occurred

user_stories.i_want: to switch between Product and each feature
user_stories.so_that: I audit a feature's own chain, not an aggregate

acceptance_criteria.given: project with features f1 and f2 holding distinct TC fixtures
acceptance_criteria.then: the Test Cases tab shows exactly f1's TCs (ids from f1's 03_TEST_CASES.json)

user_stories.i_want: the deploy verdict with its reasons on demand
user_stories.so_that: I see deploy-readiness as Aitri computes it, without polling cost

acceptance_criteria.given: a fixture project whose validate --json returns a blocked verdict with 2 reasons
acceptance_criteria.then: the panel renders the verdict and both reasons with their commands

acceptance_criteria.given: the detail view stays open through 3 collection cycles
acceptance_criteria.then: zero validate spawns occur from the cycle; only the on-open/on-refresh spawns exist

user_stories.i_want: a filterable per-TC table with evidence and manual reasons
user_stories.so_that: I work failed and pending TCs directly

acceptance_criteria.given: scope fixture with 5 TCs: 3 pass, 1 fail, 1 pending manual
acceptance_criteria.then: 5 rows with correct statuses; counts strip 3/1/1; manual-pending banner names the TC
exactly the failing TC row remains visible

user_stories.i_want: uncovered MUST requirements pinned and red
user_stories.so_that: nothing MUST ships untested without me seeing it

acceptance_criteria.given: fixture where FR-002 (MUST) has no passing test
acceptance_criteria.then: FR-002 is the first row with an 'uncovered' red marker; covered FRs show their TCs and latest results

user_stories.i_want: blocking bugs with their resolution trail
user_stories.so_that: I audit de-blocking evidence, not just counts

acceptance_criteria.given: fixture with a critical active bug carrying resolution text and a linked TC
acceptance_criteria.then: the bug is pinned with a red band; resolution and linked TC are readable
the parse-error state names the scope and says bugs are NOT counted; no empty table

user_stories.so_that: I review requirements and design without an editor

acceptance_criteria.then: the PRD shows as an FR table with ACs and the design doc renders formatted markdown; raw views are collapsed toggles
the script text appears inert/escaped; no dialog fires (no HTML injection)

user_stories.i_want: clear guidance when evidence is missing
user_stories.so_that: a mid-pipeline project tells me what to run next instead of showing blanks

acceptance_criteria.then: TCs show 'pending' and the state names 'aitri verify-run'
a degraded state names the file; no crash, no blank panel

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The overview page and its data path are unchanged: cards, triage, header tiles, 5s dashboard.json polling and its schema (additive-only).
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Existing overview/web test suites pass with unmodified assertions; dashboard.json type-inventory diff is empty for an unchanged fixture.

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Collection-cycle process budget unchanged: one aitri status --json per eligible project per cycle; the workspace adds zero per-cycle processes (validate is user-navigation-driven only).
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Instrumented run over 3 cycles with a detail view open shows zero validate/extra spawns from the cycle (FR-054 AC-2 test).

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every new endpoint enforces the loopback guard (403 non-loopback) and reads only whitelisted artifact filenames through path-confined resolution (layoutBase/resolveArtifact); the validate endpoint spawns aitri with execFile semantics (no shell) and only the fixed argument form.
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Unit/integration tests: non-loopback 403, hostile scope 400 with zero out-of-root reads, whitelist enforcement, and the validate spawn is execFile('aitri',['validate','--json'],...) — no shell string interpolation (asserted via spawn instrumentation).
Existing admin-api e2e suite passes with unmodified assertions.

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The detail endpoint responds from local disk in ≤500ms for a project with ≤200 TCs and ≤10 features; validate runs async with a visible loading state and never blocks tab rendering.
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Integration test times the detail endpoint on a 200-TC fixture (≤500ms on CI hardware, generous margin); the Summary tab renders its non-verdict content before validate resolves (loading state asserted in a component test).

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The full declared suite (unit + integration + web + e2e), including this feature's tests, runs on every push to main.
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Existing workflow globs pick up the new test files; a deliberate failing test on a branch fails CI (verified once during development).

no_go_zone: No visual restyle — slate palette/Inter/light tokens are Layer 1 (separate feature); the workspace ships in the current theme
No writes of any kind: no bug state changes, no TC verification, no approvals, no aitri command execution beyond read-only status --json / --version and the on-demand validate --json
No validate --json in the 5s collection cycle — on-demand only (process-budget decision carried from contract-catchup-rc159 / audit GAP-1)
No Cytoscape FR→TC graph in v1 — enters as a secondary visualization after the table-based Traceability tab proves itself
No GitHub API metrics, dependency scanning, or runtime monitoring (owner cut, 2026-07-05)
No arbitrary file serving: detail endpoints never open a path derived from URL input beyond registered project id + validated scope + whitelisted artifact names
No new runtime dependency without an explicit phase-2 ADR (markdown rendering: prefer a minimal in-repo renderer or an ADR-justified, sanitizing library — FR-058 AC-4 holds either way)
constraints: Server code: Node built-ins only (parent constraint); web/ additions follow the existing React 18 + Vite stack
Detail data is served on demand by new endpoints — dashboard.json stays lean (architecture detail finalized in phase 2 ADR)
validate --json cadence: on first open of the Summary verdict panel + explicit refresh, 60s server-side cache (owner-accepted assumption)

idea_provenance_sources.problem: Owner vision stated in chat 2026-07-05 ('enviar todo el informe y artefactos de QA'); seed reviewed and approved by owner in chat 2026-07-06
idea_provenance_sources.users: Owner named QA testers/auditors primary, BA/PO/PM secondary (chat 2026-07-05); restated in the approved seed
idea_provenance_sources.baseline: Current Hub behavior read from the code: aggregate-only cards, no detail route, evidence unreachable without an editor
idea_provenance_sources.success_metric: Given/When/Then criteria in the seed FEATURE_IDEA.md, presented to and approved by the owner in chat 2026-07-06
idea_provenance_sources.no_go_zone: Owner scope decisions 2026-07-05/06: Aitri-data-only, restyle separate, graph deferred, read-only; seed approved as written

coverage_map.need: Card click opens per-project detail route with back navigation and not-found handling
Header strip: name, status, verdict chip, version, artifactsDir
Detail data served on demand by localhost-only endpoints, path-confined, whitelisted
On-demand validate --json endpoint (never per-cycle)
Scope selector Product|features re-rendering tabs from each scope's own chain
Summary: per-phase status + per-feature indicator table
Summary: health score for the selected project (Layer-2 spec Tab 1)
Summary: deploy verdict rendered per VALIDATE_JSON.md with reasons/advisories/commands
Test Cases table with automation/manual_reason, status, evidence, downgraded_from, FR/AC links
Test Cases filters + counts strip + pending-manual callout
Traceability rows FR→TC→latest result; uncovered MUST pinned red
ac_coverage per FR where present
coverage_map rendered with audit freshness stamp
Bugs table with severity/status/resolution/files_changed/linked TC; blocking pinned
Corrupt BUGS.json shown inline as parse error, never zero bugs
Markdown artifacts rendered; PRD as human table; raw JSON collapsible
Artifact chain presence/absence listing
Empty/degraded states naming the producing aitri command on every tab
Overview + polling + admin + dashboard.json untouched
Process budget: no per-cycle additions
Loopback guard + path confinement + no-shell spawn on new endpoints
Admin CRUD unchanged
Detail reads fast; validate async with loading state
Visual restyle
FR→TC graph visualization
Any write/edit capability from the workspace

original_brief:
## Feature
QA Workspace: a per-project detail view (route `/project/:id`) with QA-grade
drill-down — test cases, FR→TC traceability, bugs, rendered artifacts, and the
deploy verdict — scoped by a Product | feature selector.

## Problem / Why
The Hub today is a monitoring card grid: every QA-relevant number is an
AGGREGATE (tests 89/92, bugs 3, coverage 14/16 FRs). The primary target users —
QA testers and QA auditors — cannot answer their actual working questions from
it: WHICH test case failed and with what evidence, WHICH MUST requirement lacks
a passing test, WHAT is the resolution trail of a blocking bug, WHAT does the
requirement actually say. Aitri already produces every one of those answers
(03_TEST_CASES.json, 04_TEST_RESULTS.json, 05_TRACEABILITY.json, BUGS.json,
`validate --json`, and the markdown artifact chain) — the Hub summarizes it
into counters and throws the detail away. This layer is the product's core
value for its declared target (owner vision 2026-07-05: "enviar todo el informe
y artefactos de QA que el equipo de QAs necesite para su gestión") and the #1
design priority carried from Core's close-out: make the pipeline's value
VISIBLE, not just monitored. Full spec: `idea_context/UI_UX_SPEC_V2.md`,
section "Layer 2 — QA Workspace".

## Target Users
Primary: QA testers and QA auditors reviewing an Aitri project's evidence for
sign-off. Secondary: BA / PO / PM reading the spec chain (PRD, UX spec, system
design, audit reports) without opening an editor; developers tracing an
uncovered requirement. All roles confirmed by the owner (2026-07-05).

## New Behavior
- The system must open a per-project detail view when a project card is clicked
  (route `/project/:id`), with a header strip: project name, status, deploy
  verdict, aitri version, artifactsDir.
- The system must provide a scope selector `Product | <feature>…` — each
  feature is a full pipeline with its own artifact chain under
  `features/<name>/`; selecting a scope re-renders the data tabs from that
  scope's chain.
- Tab Summary: per-phase pipeline status (approved/completed/drifted), the
  deploy verdict rendered from `aitri validate --json` (verdict, blocking
  reasons, advisories, each with its suggested command), and the per-feature
  indicator table (phase, verify, tests, bugs per feature).
- Tab Test Cases: table from 03_TEST_CASES.json × 04_TEST_RESULTS.json — TC id,
  title, automation (auto/manual + manual_reason), latest status
  (pass/fail/pending/skipped), evidence reference, downgraded_from trail,
  linked FR/AC ids; filters by status/automation/linked FR; counts strip;
  pending manual TCs called out prominently (they block coverage).
- Tab Traceability: one row per FR (id, title, priority) with its TCs and each
  TC's latest result, from 05_TRACEABILITY.json + fr_coverage; uncovered MUST
  FRs pinned top in red; ac_coverage per FR where present; intent coverage
  (coverage_map) rendered with its audit-freshness stamp.
- Tab Bugs: table from BUGS.json — id, title, severity, status, blocking flag,
  resolution, files_changed, linked TC; blocking bugs pinned with a red band;
  parse errors surfaced inline (never "0 bugs" over a corrupt file).
- Tab Artifacts: rendered reading view of the chain — markdown artifacts
  (00_DISCOVERY, 01_UX_SPEC, 02_SYSTEM_DESIGN, 04_CODE_REVIEW, AUDIT_REPORT)
  rendered as markdown; JSON artifacts (01_REQUIREMENTS as a PRD table) get a
  human projection plus a collapsible raw view.
- Every tab must have an explanatory empty/degraded state naming the aitri
  command that produces the missing artifact — never a blank panel.
- `validate --json` runs ON-DEMAND when the Summary tab is opened (never in the
  5s collection cycle — process-budget decision recorded in
  contract-catchup-rc159's no_go_zone resolving audit GAP-1). [ASSUMPTION:
  on-open with a manual refresh button and a short cache is the right cadence —
  implementer's call within phase 2.]

## Success Criteria
- Given a project with a failing TC, when the QA auditor opens Test Cases, then
  the failing TC's id, title and latest status are visible and filterable
  within 2 clicks from the overview card.
- Given a MUST FR with no passing test, when Traceability opens, then that FR
  appears pinned at the top marked uncovered (red), with its priority visible.
- Given a project with a blocking bug carrying a resolution, when Bugs opens,
  then the bug's severity, status, resolution text and linked TC are readable
  without leaving the Hub.
- Given a feature sub-pipeline selected in the scope selector, when any data
  tab renders, then its content comes from `features/<name>/`'s own artifact
  chain (verifiable with distinct fixture data per scope).
- Given a project whose artifacts are absent (e.g. Phase 3 not run), when the
  corresponding tab opens, then an empty state names the artifact and the aitri
  command that produces it (no blank panel, no crash).
- Given the Summary tab, when it opens on a validate-capable project, then the
  deploy verdict and its blocking reasons render from `aitri validate --json`
  per the VALIDATE_JSON.md contract.

## Touch Points
Modifies: `web/src/App.jsx` (routing — currently a ~30-line useRoute handling
`/` and `/admin` only), `ProjectCard.jsx` (card click → navigate). Adds: detail
view components (tabs), a data source for per-project detail — [ASSUMPTION:
the 5s dashboard.json stays lean; detail data is served on demand by new
localhost-only read endpoints on the existing web.js server (e.g.
/api/project/:id/detail), same 127.0.0.1 guard — architecture decision for
phase 2, including how `validate --json` is invoked on demand]. Server:
`lib/commands/web.js` (new read-only routes), possibly a new
`lib/collector/detail-reader.js` reading through the rc.159 readers
(resolveArtifact/layoutBase from contract-catchup-rc159).

## Must Not Break (Regression Boundary)
- The overview page (`/`) renders exactly as today: cards, triage, header
  tiles, 5s polling of dashboard.json.
- The collection cycle's process budget: one `aitri status --json` per eligible
  project per cycle — the QA Workspace adds NO per-cycle process (validate runs
  on-demand only, per user navigation).
- Localhost-only guard: every new endpoint refuses non-loopback requests with
  403, same as /api/projects (web.js remoteAddress guard).
- dashboard.json shape stays additive (the SPA overview is its consumer).
- Admin panel (`/admin`) CRUD unchanged.
- Path safety: all per-project file reads stay confined to the project root
  (reuse layoutBase/resolveArtifact; no path built from URL input without
  whitelisting to the known artifact names).

## Out of Scope
- The visual restyle (slate palette, Inter typography, light tokens) — Layer 1
  of UI_UX_SPEC_V2.md, separate feature; the workspace ships in the current
  theme.
- Editing anything: the workspace is read-only; no bug state changes, no TC
  verification, no aitri command execution beyond read-only `status --json` /
  `--version` / on-demand `validate --json`.
- The Cytoscape FR→TC graph (built, unwired) — lands as a secondary
  visualization AFTER the table-based Traceability tab proves itself; not in
  this feature's v1.
- GitHub API metrics, dependency scanning, runtime monitoring (owner cut,
  2026-07-05).
- quality_gates / ac_coverage from the snapshot where Core does not yet expose
  them (HUB-CATCHUP-0705 feedback pending); the tabs read artifacts directly on
  demand, which does not depend on that.
